notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateSubscription, GetSubscription, ListSubscriptions, UpdateSubscription, DeleteSubscription, ChangeSubscriptionCompartment, and service-local GetWorkRequest/ListWorkRequests helpers are present in v65.110.0. Create returns a Subscription body plus opc-work-request-id, while update and delete return work-request headers only.

Pinned OCI SDK lifecycle contract: coarse lifecycleState is ACTIVE, INACTIVE, DELETED, and FAILED, while actionable transition phases live in lifecycleDetails including CREATED, PENDING_ACTIVATION, PROVISIONING_STARTED, PROVISIONING_COMPLETED, PROVISIONING_FAILED, UPDATING, DELETING, ACTIVE, FAILED, EXPIRED, TERMINATED, and DELETED.

Pinned provider coverage is unconfirmed. formal/sources.lock still points at github.com/oracle/terraform-provider-oci@eb653febb1bab4cc6650a96d404a8baf36fdf671, but the accessible provider inventory did not expose a matching SELF Subscription resource or data source. The providerResource and datasource names therefore stay provisional OCI-style placeholders so generator-owned registry artifacts can derive stable links without claiming verified provider coverage.

Repo-authored runtime is narrower than the imported SDK surface: only displayName, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. ChangeSubscriptionCompartment remains out of scope, and compartmentId, tenantId, sellerId, productId, subscriptionDetails, sourceType, additionalDetails, realm, and region remain replacement-only drift.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create is bounded: existing-before-create lookup runs only when compartmentId and displayName are both present, lists by exact compartmentId plus displayName, narrows candidates by tenantId, sellerId, and productId, then rereads with GetSubscription before reusing a unique candidate in a reusable lifecycle.

Delete confirmation is workrequest-aware and required. The controller resumes delete work requests through GetWorkRequest and keeps the finalizer until GetSubscription or list fallback confirms lifecycleDetails DELETED or NotFound.
The self/Subscription rollout must remain explicitly group-qualified and distinct from ons/Subscription across formal, docs, package, and catalog surfaces.
